what_it_does: Records the design, implementation, and verification status of an L4 channel-post composer in the RightHand panel that lets an operator post back into the fabric via forkVBrain.postToChannel.
observations: Status is 'attempted-unverified': built, form-verified, and fail-soft-verified, but the success path was never confirmed.
The composer uses a channel-name picker (human names, '__test__' filtered) and an ungated message post to forkVBrain.postToChannel(name, msg).
FORM verification passed in both viewports; end-to-end wire verification only covered the fail-soft render path.
The success path is blocked by a two-store split: test channels were created in the cc_channel store instead of the bridge's fold_channels store, making them dead-letter or archived.
An owed verification task remains: post to an active, membered bridge-store channel and capture the success transparency in both viewports.
Explicitly warns not to read fail-soft verification as success verification.
standouts: The success-land was never verified because test channels lived in the wrong store.
The file is structured as a cautionary deliverable record rather than a completed verification.
It clearly separates 'verified' form/fail-soft claims from 'attempted-unverified' success claims.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, timestamps, history, and links to another board item.
Follows a HARVEST/projection lane naming convention.
Sections are standardized: STATUS, LANE, ABOUT, KIND, SUMMARY, CLAIMS/DECISIONS, RELATIONS, OPEN_QUESTIONS/GAPS, SOURCE_ADDRESS.
References external source addresses (commit hash and doc filename).
concerns: The item is marked 'built' but the critical success path remains unverified.
Test channels were created in the wrong store, suggesting environment or setup confusion.
No screenshots, logs, or other evidence are attached to substantiate the verified claims.
notes: A living guide noting the L4 channel-post composer is built and fail-soft verified, but the real success path of posting to an active bridge-store channel remains unverified and owed.
questions: Which channels are actually active and membered in the bridge's fold_channels store?
What does the success-transparency render look like in concrete terms?
Has the owed verification been completed since this item was filed?
What do commit 08209b9 and OPERATOR-SURFACE-LOOP.md contain beyond the references here?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the composer deliverable and its verification state; the content matches, with strong emphasis on the unverified success path.
apparent_themes: Operator surface loop and posting back into the fabric
Verification discipline and distinguishing fail-soft from success
intention_signals: To track a built-but-not-fully-validated feature, prevent false confidence, and record the exact remaining real-world verification step.
novelty: The explicit 'SUCCESS-LAND UNVERIFIED' framing and the root-cause attribution to a two-store channel mismatch rather than a code defect.
contribution: Keeps a precise, cautionary record of a partially verified feature, including the specific environmental blocker and the owed success-path verification step, so future work knows exactly what remains to prove.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard guide item item-b69720ab records the L4 RightHand V-post composer as built and form/fail-soft verified, with the success path of posting to an active membered bridge-store channel left unverified due to a two-store channel mismatch.
intention_for_embedding: To document the verification status and remaining real-world test obligation for the operator-facing channel-post composer.
